title: multidevice: GROUP_ADD fetches all device KeyPackages
description:
Update GROUP_ADD handling in relay/src/ws.rs to fetch all KeyPackages for the target pub_id and add each device as a separate MLS leaf.

Why: currently group_add fetches one KeyPackage per pub_id and adds one leaf. If the target user has two devices, only one gets added and the other can never decrypt group messages in this session.

What to do:
- Replace get_key_package(pub_id) with get_all_key_packages(pub_id) to get all device packages
- For each device package, call the MLS add_member path (produce a Commit + Welcome per device, or batch if the MLS layer supports it)
- Send each Welcome to the target pub_id via deliver (fan-out to all devices via the updated deliver_live)
- Handle the case where a device's KeyPackage is stale or invalid: skip that device and log a warning, do not fail the whole add
- Handle the case where get_all_key_packages returns empty: return an error to the requesting client

Acceptance: a user with two connected devices both receive a Welcome and can decrypt subsequent group messages; adding a user with one device behaves identically to current behavior.
close_reason: GetKeyPackageResult.data changed to Vec<Vec<u8>>; relay returns all devices; CLI iterates each, calls add_member per device; sealed_sender_e2e test updated
